title: anchusa leptophylla subsp. leptophylla
intro: Anchusa leptophylla subsp. leptophylla, also known as White Alkanet, is a plant species in the borage family (Boraginaceae), characterized by its white to blue-violet flowers and a hairy, square stem. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing. The health benefits of White Alkanet include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of White Alkanet include tinctures, salves, and infusions, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
